name: Baked Chimichangas
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm tortillas to 10-inches in diameter.
2. Prepare Almond Red Sauce and Jalepeno Cream Sauce; reserve.
3. Cook ground beef, onion, and garlic in a skillet over medium heat until brown. Drain.
4. Stir in remaining ingredients except tortillas, egg, and margarine. Bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Preheat oven to 500°F (260°C).
6. Spoon about half a cup of beef mixture onto the center of each tortilla.
7. Fold one end of the tortilla up about an inch over the beef mixture.
8. Fold right and lift sides over folded end, overlapping.
9. Fold remaining end down.
10. Brush edges with beaten egg to seal.
11. Place seam sides down in a greased jelly roll pan (15 1/2 x 10 1/2 x 1-inch).
12. Bake until tortillas begin to brown and filling is hot, about 8-10 minutes.
12. Serve with Almond Red Sauce and Jalepeno Cream Sauce.
Fried Chimichangas:
Omit the 2 tablespoons of butter or margarine.
Heat vegetable oil (about 1 inch) to 365°F (185°C).
Fry chimichangas in batches, turning once, until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes. Keep warm in a 300°F (150°C) oven.
